text.title: Decision Analysis Examples
text.summary: CRM selection worked example and build-vs-buy example demonstrating the six-step process with evidence requirements and sensitivity analysis.

example.note: Decision: Select CRM platform for a 12-person sales team with a $30k/year budget cap.
example.code:
# Criteria (locked 2026-04-26, weight-setter: CEO) | Criterion | Weight | Direction | |-----------------|--------|-----------------| | Feature fit | 25% | higher_better | | Total cost 3yr | 25% | lower_better | | Ease of use | 20% | higher_better | | Integration | 15% | higher_better | | Support SLA | 15% | higher_better |
# Evidence per cell (sampled) Salesforce / Feature fit: score=5 / source: https://salesforce.com/features (fetched 2026-04-26) HubSpot / Total cost: score=4 / source: https://hubspot.com/pricing (fetched 2026-04-26)
# Weighted totals Salesforce: 3.55, HubSpot: 4.00, Pipedrive: 3.85
# Sensitivity analysis output (sensitivity.py) Robustness under ±20% weight noise: HubSpot: 81.4% mean=3.99 sd=0.18 ← winner, robust (>70%) Pipedrive: 11.3% mean=3.83 sd=0.20 Salesforce: 7.3% mean=3.53 sd=0.21
# Recommendation: HubSpot Rationale: Best feature/cost balance; wins 81% of sensitivity trials. Risk: Limited Salesforce-CRM migration support. Mitigation: pilot import with 500 contacts first.

example.note: Decision: Build custom queue service or buy a managed queue (SQS/CloudAMQP). Criteria lock before scoring.
example.code:
# Criteria (locked 2026-03-15, weight-setter: CTO) | Criterion | Weight | Direction | |--------------------|--------|----------------| | Time to market | 30% | lower_better | | Total cost 3yr | 25% | lower_better | | Customization need | 25% | higher_better | | Operational risk | 20% | lower_better |
# Scoring (evidence URLs required) | Criterion | Wt | Build | Buy (SQS) | Buy (CloudAMQP) | |---------------|----|-------|-----------|-----------------| | Time to mkt | 30%| 2 | 5 | 5 | | Cost 3yr | 25%| 3 | 4 | 4 | | Customization | 25%| 5 | 2 | 3 | | Op risk | 20%| 2 | 5 | 4 | | Weighted | | 3.00 | 4.05 | 4.05 |
# Sensitivity: SQS and CloudAMQP tied (>70% each under ±20% weight noise) # Escalate to human: choose by operational preference (existing AWS stack → SQS)

antipattern.description: Analysis performed to justify a decision already made: options are scored after the winner is known, and weights are set to produce the predetermined result.
antipattern.reason: Reviewers detect post-hoc rationalization from weight patterns that perfectly match the chosen option. Trust drops and the decision process loses legitimacy for future decisions.

antipattern.description: Pure financial trade-off scored on a 1-5 scale instead of using NPV or discounted cash flow.
antipattern.reason: A "cost" score of 3 vs 4 throws away precision that financial analysis preserves. For purely financial decisions, use NPV directly; reserve 1-5 scoring for non-quantifiable criteria.
